seoTitle: How to Integrate Jira With Bitbucket
metaDescription: Learn how to link Jira and Bitbucket so commits, branches, and pull requests connect directly to your issues for smoother dev team tracking.
intro: When Jira and Bitbucket are connected, commits, branches, and pull requests can link directly to the issues they relate to, giving your team a clearer picture of development progress. This guide covers setting up that integration.

steps.heading: Confirm both tools are on the same platform or linkable
steps.body: Jira and Bitbucket integrate natively when both are part of the same Atlassian setup, so confirm your accounts are eligible for direct linking.

steps.heading: Connect Bitbucket to your Jira project
steps.body: Use the development tools or application links settings in Jira to connect your Bitbucket repository.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Log in and grant the necessary permissions so Jira can access repository activity from Bitbucket.

steps.heading: Link commits and branches to issues
steps.body: Reference a Jira issue key in your commit messages or branch names so activity automatically ties back to the relevant issue.

steps.heading: View development activity on the issue
steps.body: Once connected, check a Jira issue to see linked commits, branches, and pull requests appear directly in its development panel.

faq.question: How does Jira know which commits relate to an issue?
faq.answer: You include the Jira issue key, such as PROJ-123, in your commit message or branch name, and Jira automatically links the activity.

faq.question: Can I see pull request status directly on a Jira issue?
faq.answer: Yes, once integrated, the development panel on a Jira issue shows linked branches, commits, and pull request status.

faq.question: Does this integration require both tools to be on Atlassian Cloud?
faq.answer: Native integration is smoothest when both are Atlassian Cloud products, though other setups may require additional configuration or apps.

faq.question: What happens if I forget to include the issue key in a commit?
faq.answer: The commit simply won't link automatically to the issue, so you'd need to manually reference it or amend the commit message if possible.
